title: Identify the Known Values
text: Start by identifying the known values in the problem. What is the initial velocity of the blood? What is the final velocity? What is the distance over which the acceleration occurs?

title: Use the Kinematic Equation
text: Use the kinematic equation that relates initial velocity, final velocity, acceleration, and distance: $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$. Solve for acceleration (a).

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: Substitute the known values into the equation $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$ to find the acceleration. Remember to convert all units to meters and seconds.

title: Find the Time Using Another Kinematic Equation
text: Now that you have the acceleration, use the equation $$v_f=v_i+a t$$ to find the time (t).

title: Calculate the Time
text: Substitute the known values into the equation $$v_f=v_i+a t$$ to solve for time (t).
